itemListElement.name: Land Rover Discovery Sport L550 D150/D180/D240

mainEntity.name: How much does an Ingenium 2.0 diesel engine replacement cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned Ingenium 2.0 diesel typically costs £3,500–£5,800 supply only, varying by power output and specific sub-variant. Fitting adds £900–£1,700 depending on labour rates and vehicle model.

mainEntity.name: Is the Ingenium 2.0 diesel a reliable engine?

acceptedAnswer.text: It is a capable and efficient modern diesel (3.5★), but its reputation is mixed due to pre-2019 timing chain tensioner failures and oil-dilution from DPF regens. Post-2019 revisions are significantly improved.

mainEntity.name: What is the most common Ingenium 2.0 diesel failure?

acceptedAnswer.text: Pre-2019 timing chain tensioner failure is the most critical — it can cause sudden chain stretch and catastrophic valve/piston interference. Other common issues include EGR cooler leaks and oil dilution from incomplete DPF regenerations on short-trip vehicles.

mainEntity.name: Which Land Rover models use the Ingenium 2.0 diesel?

acceptedAnswer.text: It powers the Discovery Sport (L550), Evoque (L551), Velar (L560), and Defender (L663) — spanning 150–240 hp outputs.

mainEntity.name: Is the Land Rover Ingenium 2.0 diesel the same as Jaguar's version?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — the 204DTD and 224DT engine codes are shared across both brands. The engine is mechanically identical between Land Rover and Jaguar fitments, though ECU calibrations and ancillaries differ by model.

hero.subHeadline: The Ingenium 2.0 diesel is JLR's modern four-cylinder workhorse — pre‑2019 timing chain tensioners are the defining risk. Backed by 1,500+ real UK enquiries in 2025 [EM-VERIFIED].

rows.value: Also fitted to Jaguar (XE, XF, F‑Pace, E‑Pace)

verdictRating.confidence: 88% confidence — based on 1,500+ real UK enquiries, specialist rebuild reports, and JLR TSB documentation.

rows.score: 16 (Midpoint of Reconditioned (Supply Only) range: £3,500–£5,800 → £4,650 midpoint → falls in £3,000–£4,999 bracket → 16)
12 (Pre‑2019 tensioner risk and DPF/oil‑dilution complexity add labour; post‑2019 improved but still a modern Euro‑6 diesel)
12 (204DTD well‑supported via JLR and aftermarket; 224DT parts interchangeable; some delay for specific pre‑2019 tensioner components)
12 (Reconditioned cost (£4,650 midpoint) as % of lowest typical vehicle value (Discovery Sport ~£8,000–£12,000) = 39–58% → falls in 45–59% bracket → 12)

verdictRating.oneLineVerdict: The Ingenium 2.0 diesel is a strong, efficient modern unit — but pre‑2019 timing chain tensioners can fail without much warning — insist on full service history and post‑2019 hardware where possible.

compatibility.crossBrandNote: Also powers the Jaguar XE, XF, F‑Pace and E‑Pace → [Jaguar Ingenium 2.0 Diesel page].

costGuide.labourEstimate: Fitting an Ingenium 2.0 diesel typically takes 10–14 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer) [EM-QUOTE].
costGuide.sharedCostNote: Parts cost is identical across Land Rover and Jaguar applications; labour time varies slightly by engine bay access and model‑specific subframe removal.
